problem_statement: Autonomous coding agents operate at high velocity and their planning, tool-call sequences, and cross-file decisions stay hidden from human supervisors, so oversight based on code diffs captures only local outputs while global architectural intent silently drifts ('agentic entropy'). The paper addresses how to give humans process-level visibility to keep oversight substantive.
magnitude_evidence: Industry reports show that nearly 45% of AI-generated code fails basic security checks when users rely on prompt-driven workflows without guardrails (Veracode GenAI Code Security Report)
insufficiency_evidence: Traditional code diff-based review 'reveals only the outcome of agentic activity'; the caching example produces code that 'compiles, passes existing unit tests... and appear benign in a standard code diff' while violating architectural invariants; reviewers experience 'cognitive overload or verification fatigue' and move 'from strategic supervision to passive rubber-stamping' (reviewer's paradox)
solution_mechanisms: reasoning monitoring of tool calls and plans
deviation detector comparing intent vs seed
cognitive debt index tracking reviewer engagement
